one_time_description: Teck Highland Valley Copper Partnership payment of $4,040,000 with no prior year comparative, appears to be a non-recurring receipt
economic_notes: Subsequent to year end, First Nation entered into agreement with Province of BC for a large multi-plex housing project with $15,000,000 in funding held in escrow. Four government business enterprises: Swl'eps Operating Corporation, Swl'eps Limited Partnership, 9086 Investments Ltd., and Nimimhl Tmixw Holding Corporation.
notes: Fiscal year is 2023 (year ended March 31, 2023), not 2024. Prior year (2022) financial statements were audited by a different auditor who expressed a qualified opinion. The $4,040,000 from Teck Highland Valley Copper Partnership is a significant one-time item with no budget or prior year amount. Long-term debt includes both long-term debt ($5,377,216) and capital lease obligations ($433,472) totalling $5,810,688. Annual debt service calculated as repayment of long-term debt ($1,425,600) plus repayments of capital lease obligations ($77,672) = $1,503,272. Four GBEs accounted for under modified equity method. No schedule of remuneration was included in the financial statements. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($3,031,557) + Province of BC ($1,838,183) + CMHC ($1,593,339) + Other Federal Funding ($225,425) + First Nation Health Authority ($44480) + Specific claims funding ($143,789) + BCFN Gaming Revenue Sharing LP ($342,431) + Citxw Nlaka'pamux Assembly ($6,000) + Teck HVC ($4,040,000 treated as government/corporate transfer) = note: Teck HVC excluded from government transfers as it is a mining partnership, revised total_government_transfers excludes Teck: $7,225,204 but Teck included in OSR calculation.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
